herbs.herb_desc: The capsule of Aesculus hippocastanum, also known as the horse chestnut tree, is a spiky, green or brown, and leathery covering that encloses the seed. It splits open at maturity, typically in late summer or early fall, to reveal 3-4 large, brown seeds. The capsule remains on the tree until the seeds are fully mature and dry, at which point it will fall off, dispersing the seeds to the ground.
